candidates.source_theme: APF as self-hosted playbook/process authoring system
candidates.m62_3_decision: Document as the parent pattern; do not promote to runtime core.
candidates.rationale: It describes the authoring experience, not a low-level runtime instruction.

candidates.source_theme: Inputs may be required, optional, absent, deferred, or produced later
candidates.future_home: language package authoring standard; possible lint candidate
candidates.m62_3_decision: Formalize first as state/schema convention, not opcode.
candidates.rationale: Many packages need the concept, but compiler behavior is not required yet.

candidates.source_theme: Outputs can be known, partial, AI-proposed, optional, deferred, or absent
candidates.m62_3_decision: Keep as candidate catalog model until several modules use it.
candidates.rationale: Good reusable structure, but not enough evidence for IR object yet.

candidates.source_theme: User chooses outputs at each terminal point
candidates.future_home: reusable applied-module subflow; possible authoring DSL pattern
candidates.m62_3_decision: Treat as APF subflow before formal language construct.
candidates.rationale: It is a guided review interaction, not a runtime validation primitive by itself.

candidates.source_theme: Terminal paths bind selected outputs/templates
candidates.m62_3_decision: Document as reusable subflow; defer opcode/IR promotion.
candidates.rationale: Binding semantics need APF implementation evidence first.

candidates.source_theme: Check template exists, is verified, and can be filled on the path
candidates.future_home: possible lint/check rule after APF patch
candidates.m62_3_decision: Plan as APF subflow; only later evaluate lint rule.
candidates.rationale: Needs concrete template metadata conventions first.

candidates.source_theme: Draft missing template when new output is selected
candidates.m62_3_decision: Keep APF-local until template lifecycle stabilizes.
candidates.rationale: Useful workflow, but too application-facing for core IR now.

candidates.source_theme: Mock-filled examples are required for human template review
candidates.future_home: output template documentation standard; possible validation checklist
candidates.m62_3_decision: Adopt as documentation/artifact standard before automation.
candidates.rationale: Human review value is immediate; enforcement can wait.

candidates.source_theme: User approves/corrects/defers/removes template
candidates.m62_3_decision: Keep as guided APF subflow.
candidates.rationale: Review decisions should be proven in APF before language promotion.

candidates.source_theme: Terminal path is not ready without terminal/output/template/readiness decisions
candidates.m62_3_decision: Mark as first serious future lint candidate, not active rule.
candidates.rationale: Readiness is important, but current packages do not all have required metadata yet.

candidates.source_theme: User can provide root-only, shallow subtree, deep subtree, full draft, or free-form logic
candidates.m62_3_decision: Promote as documented authoring pattern, not runtime construct.
candidates.rationale: It shapes interaction flow but does not require core runtime behavior.

candidates.source_theme: Capture how much of the tree the user currently sees
candidates.m62_3_decision: Keep APF-local until branch 1/2 review closes.
candidates.rationale: Depends on the APF dialogue sequence currently under review.

candidates.source_theme: AI generates draft subtree from user vision
candidates.m62_3_decision: Keep as APF subflow with explicit draft != approved rule.
candidates.rationale: Needs human review status model before formalization.

candidates.source_theme: Start standard node/branch review after draft generation
candidates.m62_3_decision: Document as transition point in APF.
candidates.rationale: Primarily orchestration within authoring module.

candidates.source_theme: Show node alias, description, state, gates, branches, risks and decision
candidates.m62_3_decision: Use as APF-level reusable subflow; do not create opcode.
candidates.rationale: Human review surface, not core runtime operation.

candidates.source_theme: Review branch-by-branch and track siblings/deferred returns
candidates.m62_3_decision: Use as APF-level reusable subflow.
candidates.rationale: Requires APF review bookkeeping before broader reuse.

candidates.source_theme: After node review, decide terminal / continue / intermediate / rebuild / defer
candidates.m62_3_decision: Treat as required APF patch item, not language core.
candidates.rationale: This fixes authoring dialogue ambiguity.

candidates.source_theme: Current mode/node/state/gates/outputs/siblings/expected decision must be visible
candidates.m62_3_decision: Promote as review rendering standard, not IR object.
candidates.rationale: It is a user-facing presentation contract.

candidates.source_theme: Separate selected actions, not-selected actions, blocked actions, siblings, deferred returns
candidates.future_home: review-state schema convention; possible lint checklist
candidates.m62_3_decision: Formalize in APF review state first.
candidates.rationale: Prevents semantic confusion, but requires consistent state fields before enforcement.

candidates.source_theme: Branch-specific flow joins shared full-validation/handoff tail
candidates.future_home: language flow modeling candidate; possible compiler/lint support
candidates.m62_3_decision: Treat as highest-value future IR candidate, but not in M62.3.
candidates.rationale: Likely needs formal semantics because duplicated shared tails are a structural problem.

candidates.source_theme: Explicit reference to shared validation/handoff tail
candidates.m62_3_decision: Pair with FLOW.JOIN for future design.
candidates.rationale: Needs careful compatibility with existing YAML/IR edge model.

candidates.source_theme: Apply scoped patches after closure and minimal validation
candidates.m62_3_decision: Adopt as policy/checklist, not runtime operation.
candidates.rationale: It describes developer discipline and review gates.

candidates.source_theme: Run YAML/lint/compile/test after scoped patches
candidates.m62_3_decision: Keep aligned with existing Ordo validation discipline.
candidates.rationale: Already consistent with M60/M61 milestone practice.

candidates.source_theme: Full validation deferred to terminal/pre-handoff gate
candidates.future_home: package lifecycle docs; possible validation profile
candidates.m62_3_decision: Document as lifecycle rule; do not enforce globally yet.
candidates.rationale: Full validation requirements vary by package profile.

candidates.source_theme: User can say the process itself needs to change during execution/review
candidates.m62_3_decision: Keep as future APF subflow until branch 4 evidence is broader.
candidates.rationale: Good pattern but currently tied to APF correction branch.

candidates.source_theme: Show proposed process change with before/after
candidates.m62_3_decision: Keep paired with PROCESS.FEEDBACK.CAPTURE.
candidates.rationale: Needs before/after representation standard.

candidates.source_theme: Apply approved scoped change and resume
candidates.m62_3_decision: Keep APF-local until scoped patch representation is stable.
candidates.rationale: Potentially risky if generalized prematurely.

candidates.source_theme: Graph rendering is on demand, context-focused by default, not validation source of truth
candidates.m62_3_decision: Document only; already supported by Visual Graph utility.
candidates.rationale: Rendering policy is a workflow rule, not runtime semantics.

candidates.source_theme: YAML parse/lint/compile/human review/full validation/handoff are distinct statuses
candidates.future_home: package lifecycle status convention; possible docs/lint support
candidates.m62_3_decision: Formalize as status vocabulary in docs first.
candidates.rationale: Prevents false readiness claims; enforcement can come later.
